name: Property Grid
description: Dashboard grid of all properties with filtering, search, view toggles, and portfolio summary.

cards.description: Card grid layout with property cards

slots.header: Title, add property button, view toggle

map.description: Interactive map view with property location pins and a scrollable sidebar property list

visual_brief: Dashboard page for all properties. Header row with 'Properties' title, a search input, and an 'Add Property' button. A portfolio summary bar shows total properties, total units, average occupancy, and total monthly revenue. Filter controls below offer search by name, filter by status, and view toggle (cards/table/map). The main area displays property cards in a responsive grid, a data table, or a map view with pins. Pagination controls at the bottom handle large portfolios.

responsive.mobile: Single-column property cards. Summary metrics in a 2x2 grid. Filters collapse into a filter button. Map view takes full screen.
responsive.tablet: Two-column card grid. Summary in a row. Filters visible. Table view has horizontal scroll.
responsive.desktop: Three-column card grid or full-width table. All summary, filters, and pagination visible.

layout_hints.portfolio_priority: Lead with portfolio-wide scanability first: summary metrics, active filters, then the properties themselves. The user should understand the state of the portfolio before diving into any one asset.
layout_hints.view_modes: Cards, table, and map should preserve the same property hierarchy and filter context rather than feeling like unrelated screens.
layout_hints.toolbar_rhythm: Search, filters, and add-property actions belong in one operational header band. Parent pages should not wrap this pattern in another competing control bar.

motion.micro: View toggles and property cards may sharpen or lift subtly on hover. Filter chips or selectors should respond crisply without stealing attention from the portfolio items.
motion.transitions: Changing between card, table, and map modes should preserve orientation and current filter state rather than redrawing the whole surface abruptly.

keyboard: Header controls, filters, and property items should follow a predictable top-to-bottom, left-to-right order.
announcements: Portfolio summaries, active filters, and individual property identity should be exposed semantically so the collection remains understandable beyond its visual layout.

composition.PropertyGrid: Container(d-section, flex-col, gap-6) > [Header + PortfolioSummary + Filters + PropertyItems + Pagination]
